title: oxyceros longiflorus var. major
intro: Oxyceros longiflorus var. major, also known as the Himalayan Bignonia, is a flowering evergreen plant native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides in its leaves, stems, and roots. The medicinal properties of Oxyceros longiflorus var. major have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine in the regions where it is native.
